Off Calendar Sep-23-2008 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-02-2008 Continued To Sep-23-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rudolph Vasquez for and with Nelson Paredes.

Mr. Vasquez stated that he was ready to close the estate. The matter was continued to September 23, 2008 for filing of the Petition For Final Distribution.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Reset Amended Petition For Probate
Off Calendar Apr-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Rudolph Vasquez for and with Nelson Paredes.

The Court stated that there was no proof of Mr. Paredes being the grandnephew. Mr. Vasquez stated that he had made every effort, that there were too many missing elements, that the money was in a blocked account and that he was ready to close the estate. In addition, he stated that that the will needed to be admitted, that he could file a petition by next week and that he needed guidance on how to distribute the assets to the minors in El Salvador. Mr. Ferdon suggested the depositing the funds into a blocked here until the minors reached the age of 18 and that the domicile state needed to be determined. The probate Examiner informed the parties that the Special Administrator could not distribute the assets for four months after the issuance of the letters. The will was admitted. The order appointing Nelson Paredes was signed in open court. The matter was set for status on the Petition For Final Distribution to be heard on September 2, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Rafael Cabrera.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Receipt For Blocked Account
Off Calendar Feb-19-2008 Off Calendar

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (44 Ney Street S.F.)
Off Calendar Jan-29-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rudolph Vasquez for the Petitioner.

Mr. Vasquez presented a copy of the appraisal to the Court. The sale was confirmed at $480,000.00. The proceeds are to be deposited into a blocked account. Status on filing the receipt for the blocked account is set for February 19, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-31-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Rudolph Vasquez for and with Nelson Paredes (Petitioner).

Mr. Vasquez reported that he had not been able to obtain the birth certificate from El Salvador, that he was not sure where the father was born, and that he did not have any additional evidence to support Mr. Nelson's position. Mr. Ferdon expressed that the property needed to be taken care of now, that the proceeding with the sale of the property to prevent forclosure should take priority, and that he was not prepared to take the matter. The Court signed the Order appointing Mr. Nelson as the Special Administrator with general powers to cure all defects with respect to the 44 Ney Street property. He may continue to make payments, and the sale of the property must be confirmed through the Court. The bond is set at $12,000.00.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Doug Handa.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-12-2007 Continued To Oct-31-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Rudolph Vasquez appearing for and with petitoner Nelson Paredes. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for the Public Administrator.
Mr. Vasquez stated that Nelson is the grand nephew of the decedent. He informed the Court that he is still making an effort to obtain certified documents from El Salvador. The Court stated that Mr. Vasquez had not provided enough proof to have the petitioner appointed. Mr. Ferdon suggested that Mr. Vasquez provide a plan as to how the property will be maintained. The Court continued the matter to 10/31/07.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Bailiff: A. Aguerre. Clerk: Rosie Noguera
